Title: Invalid URL redirects to login instead of showing Page not found
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Log out if logged in 2. Visit https://auction.hiring.kgportal.com/non-existent-page-404 3. Observe page content
Expected Result: User sees ""Page not found"" or 404 message per requirement.
Actual Result: User is redirected to login page (/login). No ""Page not found"" message is displayed.

Title: No visible Log out / Sign out control on landing or campaign pages
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Log in with user@example.com 2. On Campaign Landing Page look for Log out / Sign out / user menu 3. Navigate to a campaign and repeat
Expected Result: Clearly visible Log out or Sign out control (or user menu containing it) so user can log out and clear session.
Actual Result: No button or link with text ""Log out"" or ""Sign out"" found. User menu or profile control may be absent or use non-standard labeling; logout flow cannot be verified.

Title: Accessibility: Color contrast violations (AXE)
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Log in 2. Open Campaign Landing Page or a campaign items page 3. Run AXE (browser extension or DevTools) with WCAG 2/2.1 AA rules
Expected Result: Zero AXE violations; No AXE violations per requirement.
Actual Result: AXE reports color-contrast violations on multiple nodes (e.g. 10 nodes on campaign/bidding pages). Fails WCAG AA contrast requirements.

Title: Accessibility: aria-hidden-focus violation (AXE)
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Log in 2. Open a campaign items page 3. Run AXE with default or WCAG rules
Expected Result: No focusable elements inside aria-hidden regions.
Actual Result: AXE reports aria-hidden-focus: focusable element present in aria-hidden subtree (e.g. in modal or dropdown).

Title: Failed login does not always show explicit error message
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Go to login 2. Enter user@example.com and wrong password (e.g. WrongPassword123) 3. Click Sign in
Expected Result: System displays appropriate error message for failed authentication per requirement.
Actual Result: User may remain on login page with no visible error message or feedback. Requirement states system should handle and display appropriate error messages for failed authentication.

Title: Auth keys remain in localStorage when logout control not used
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Log in 2. Do not use logout (or if no logout control skip) 3. Open DevTools > Application > Local Storage 4. Inspect keys matching token/auth/user/session
Expected Result: When user logs out auth token and user info are cleared from localStorage per requirement.
Actual Result: If logout control is not available or not used localStorage still contains 2+ keys matching token/auth/user/session. Requirement cannot be fully verified without a clear logout flow.
